title: Pet Class
description: Create a class called Pet with an __init__ method that accepts name and age parameters. Store them as instance attributes. Then add a method called introduce that prints exactly: 'My name is {name} and I am {age} years old'. Finally, create an instance pet = Pet('Fluffy', 3) and call pet.introduce().
starter_code:
# Define your Pet class here

# Create an instance and call introduce()
expected_output: My name is Fluffy and I am 3 years old

code_requirements.description: Defines a class using the class keyword
code_requirements.fail_message: You need to define a class using the 'class' keyword.

code_requirements.description: Has an __init__ method to set up attributes
code_requirements.fail_message: Your class needs an __init__ method to set up the name and age attributes.

code_requirements.description: Has an introduce method with self as the first parameter
code_requirements.fail_message: Define an 'introduce' method with 'self' as its first parameter.

hints: Use class Pet: to define the class, then def __init__(self, name, age): inside it
Store attributes with self.name = name and self.age = age
In the introduce method, use print(f"My name is {self.name} and I am {self.age} years old")

tests.expected_output: My name is Fluffy and I am 3 years old
